headline: Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us | Microsoft Security Blog
article_title: Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us | Microsoft Security Blog

article_items.title: Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us | Microsoft Security Blog
article_items.description: A surge in real-world attacks against agentic AI systems is reshaping how we think about risk. Based on 12 months of red teaming, this update introduces seven new failure modes, from supply chain compromise to goal hijacking, and the practical mitigations teams need now.
article_items.excerpt: A surge in real-world attacks against agentic AI systems is reshaping how we think about risk. Based on 12 months of red teaming, this update introduces seven new failure modes, from supply chain compromise to goal hijacking, and the practical mitigations teams need now.
article_items.summary: Session context contamination occurs when an adversary introduces data early in a session that biases the agent’s reasoning in subsequent steps, without triggering safety controls at any individual step. The update adds seven new failure mode categories, expands the mitigations section, and grounds the framework in 12 months of red team engagements against deployed agentic systems. Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us Products and services When the Microsoft AI Red Team published the Taxonomy of Failure Modes in Agentic AI Systems in April 2025, the goal was a shared vocabulary for a threat landscape that did not fit existing frameworks. SBOM. Controls include context provenance tracking, structured separation between trusted system context and untrusted retrieved content, session integrity monitoring for anomalous accumulation patterns, and bounded session contexts that limit how much external content can influence a session’s reasoning. OpenClaw, launched.

key_points: Session context contamination occurs when an adversary introduces data early in a session that biases the agent’s reasoning in subsequent steps, without triggering safety controls at any individual step.
The update adds seven new failure mode categories, expands the mitigations section, and grounds the framework in 12 months of red team engagements against deployed agentic systems.
OpenClaw, launched in January 2026, accumulated over 336,000 GitHub stars and spawned more than 2,100 agents within 48 hours of release.
The v1.0 taxonomy was forward-looking.
Based on 12 months of red teaming, this update introduces seven new failure modes, from supply chain compromise to goal hijacking, and the practical mitigations teams need now.
The original taxonomy covered agent compromise but did not sufficiently distinguish the mechanism of compromise from the strategic objective of redirecting the agent’s goal state.
Why the Taxonomy Needed Updating Open-source agentic frameworks went mainstream faster than the security community was ready for.
The failure modes added in v2.0 are the ones that twelve months of operational data made compelling enough to include.
A security audit conducted shortly after launch identified 512 vulnerabilities including CVE-2026-25253, a one-click RCE via WebSocket hijacking.

Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us Updating the taxonomy of failure modes in agentic AI systems: What a year of red teaming taught us Products and services.
The Model Context Protocol became the de facto standard for connecting models to external tools.
The updated whitepaper is available now .

article_items.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.
article_items.excerpt: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.
article_items.summary: The news of Poke’s launch on Apple’s Messages for Business comes just days ahead of Apple’s anticipated Worldwide Developers Conference on Monday, where it’s expected to introduce an AI-optimized version of Siri along with other AI tools and services for app developers. Launched in March, Poke is one of the first AI agents designed to be accessible to everyday users who don’t have the technical skill set or inclination to work with command-line tools or more complex agentic systems, like OpenClaw. More from TechCrunch Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform Poke , a startup that turns using AI agents into something as simple as sending a text message, has become the first AI agent approved to run on Apple’s Messages for Business platform . Benchmarks measure the performance of models in a given domain, and they’re “saturated” when models achieve close to 100% performance.

article_items.title: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety: Customizable Multimodal Safety for Global Enterprise AI
article_items.description: A Blog post by NVIDIA on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by NVIDIA on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: Compared to another reasoning safety model, our model generated up to 50% fewer tokens when reasoning is enabled, making it efficient in terms of cost and latency. Reasoning allows a content safety model to dynamically interpret and enforce custom, domain-specific policies defined in natural language at the time of inference. This consistency helps teams apply the same safety posture across customer, employee, and partner-facing workflows instead of relying on English-only moderation or separate regional safety models. Policy Iteration: The traces reveal how the model interprets edge cases, allowing teams to iteratively refine and improve custom policy language. Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety achieves 3x lower end-to-end latency on a multimodal benchmark compared to an alternative multimodal safety model.
article_items.what_happened: Compared to another reasoning safety model, our model generated up to 50% fewer tokens when reasoning is enabled, making it efficient in terms of cost and latency. Reasoning allows a content safety model to dynamically interpret and enforce custom, domain-specific policies defined in natural language at the time of inference.
article_items.why_matters: This consistency helps teams apply the same safety posture across customer, employee, and partner-facing workflows instead of relying on English-only moderation or separate regional safety models. key_points: The Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety Dataset is multimodal, multilingual, and includes safety reasoning traces that were used to train the model.
Compared to another reasoning safety model, our model generated up to 50% fewer tokens when reasoning is enabled, making it efficient in terms of cost and latency.
Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety achieves 3x lower end-to-end latency on a multimodal benchmark compared to an alternative multimodal safety model.
Reasoning allows a content safety model to dynamically interpret and enforce custom, domain-specific policies defined in natural language at the time of inference.

A Blog post by NVIDIA on Hugging Face Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety: Customizable Multimodal Safety for Global Enterprise AI This post covers what changes in 3.5, the design decisions behind each new capability, and how.
This extends the work first introduced in Nemotron Content Safety Reasoning 4B to the full multimodal, multilingual setting.
Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety is available on Hugging Face under the NVIDIA Open Model License for research and commercial use, along with the training dataset .
These reasoning traces were generated in a 2-step manner to make them concise, similar to the Nemotron Content Safety Reasoning 4B model.
Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety delivers strong harmful-content classification accuracy across multilingual and multimodal safety benchmarks, averaging about 85% across the evaluated benchmark set.
Unified Multimodal Evaluation Nemotron 3 introduced image understanding; Nemotron 3.5 deepens the multimodal integration.
Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety's compact 4B design helps reduce the cost and latency of repeated safety checks, making multilingual and multimodal guardrails practical for real-world AI applications.
The dataset driving Nemotron 3.5 is an evolution of the multimodal, multilingual blends used for Nemotron 3, with additions targeting the reasoning and custom-policy capabilities.

article_items.title: Nvidia Unveils New physical AI Research and Agent Workflows
article_items.description: Nvidia Unveils New physical AI Research and Agent Workflows
article_items.excerpt: Nvidia Unveils New physical AI Research and Agent Workflows
article_items.summary: Nvidia Unveils New physical AI Research and Agent Workflows Nvidia Unveils New Physical AI Research and Agent Workflows The systems, powered by Cosmos 3, are designed to accelerate development of autonomous vehicles, robots and vision AI systems. Unveiled this week at the Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition conference in Denver, the updates build on Nvidia's recently launched Cosmos 3 world foundation model and are designed to help researchers automate key stages of physical AI development, including. In the vision AI arena, Nvidia expanded its video analysis capabilities with updates to its Metropolis platform, including tools for video search, summarization and synthetic data generation. With the updates, the company is positioning virtual environments as a critical tool for developing AI systems that can safely operate in the physical world. Nvidia has released a spate of new physical AI research tools, agent workflows and open source models to train more advanced AI systems for the real world.

article_items.title: Meta Rolls Out AI Agent for Enterprises Globally
article_items.description: Meta Rolls Out AI Agent for Enterprises Globally
article_items.excerpt: Meta Rolls Out AI Agent for Enterprises Globally
article_items.summary: In tandem with the launch of the tool, Meta is also introducing a new Business Agent Platform that provides infrastructure for enterprises using AI agents at scale. The Facebook-Instagram-WhatsApp parent company said in a June 3 release that its Business Agent enables enterprises of all sizes “to increase their output and deliver personalized experiences for customers using AI” by handling an array of tasks, including handling customer inquiries, scheduling appointments and recommending products. Related: Google’s Gemma 4 12B Shows AI Race Moving to Edge Devices Ultimately, the company claims, the goal is for the agent to effectively run all daily operations, including tasks as diverse as conducting market research, surfacing product insights and providing competitive intelligence. Deployment -- or connection into existing infrastructure -- can be done in minutes, with Meta claiming the agent can easily accommodate a. Companies interested in these future services are being encouraged to join a waitlist, but Business Agent, in its current guise, can be activated immediately and for free, although that may not be the case for long. Meta Rolls Out AI Agent for Enterprises Globally.
article_items.what_happened: In tandem with the launch of the tool, Meta is also introducing a new Business Agent Platform that provides infrastructure for enterprises using AI agents at scale. The Facebook-Instagram-WhatsApp parent company said in a June 3 release that its Business Agent enables enterprises of all sizes “to increase their output and deliver personalized experiences for customers using AI” by handling an array of tasks, including handling customer inquiries, scheduling appointments and recommending products.

key_points: In tandem with the launch of the tool, Meta is also introducing a new Business Agent Platform that provides infrastructure for enterprises using AI agents at scale.

The Facebook-Instagram-WhatsApp parent company said in a June 3 release that its Business Agent enables enterprises of all sizes “to increase their output and deliver personalized experiences for customers using AI” by.

Meta Rolls Out AI Agent for Enterprises Globally Meta Rolls Out AI Agent for Enterprises Globally The tool is aimed at small businesses and is part of the social media giant’s push beyond consumers.

Deployment -- or connection into existing infrastructure -- can be done in minutes, with Meta claiming the agent can easily accommodate a host of different languages and adopt a company’s tone of voice.
Meta says that in the coming months, access to the agent will be using paid subscription offerings with options for businesses of all sizes.
Meta has unveiled an AI tool designed to help grow companies by automating various tasks and services.
The platform connects to what Meta says is a “growing suite” of external systems, such as Shopify, Zendesk, and Shopee, where agents can take actions on behalf of the business.

The agent is already in use by around a million businesses on WhatsApp and Messenger in select markets, including Brazil, India and Mexico, but Meta is now making it available to all companies and has extended it to.
for 26 years, including spells as editor of leading consumer news website and weekly Auto Express and respected buying guide CarBuyer.

Companies interested in these future services are being encouraged to join a waitlist, but Business Agent, in its current guise, can be activated immediately and for free, although that may not be the case for long.
While the ability to interact around the clock is important for many small businesses, Meta says this feature is a starting point, with more to come.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
Tie funding, partnerships, or revenue to product direction or competitive pressure.
Avoid hype words unless the article gives evidence.
Lead with the actual product, model, or API name.
